title: Authorization, Tenancy, Object, Function, and Property Policy · Inference Brief: Authz Subject Resource Action
summary: Routes check a broad role but not object ownership, tenant filters are optional, update bodies bind protected fields, and denials leak which records exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts authz subject resource action increment in deny-by-default resource authorization decision. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.
Construct the smallest principal after full credential validation and record redacted success, failure class, rotation, revocation, and recovery evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Authz Subject Resource Action · Theory Authz Subject Resource Action
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3372 in a accessible status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.
